gaps.title: Strict publish sync gate
gaps.nextAction: Run the runtime-only non-writing strict sync gate after the intended branch is pushed.
gaps.boundary: Committed gap-register snapshots do not close strict sync; only the runtime-only non-writing strict sync audit proves current local upstream sync.

gaps.title: Owner license decision
gaps.nextAction: Owner chooses a reuse license or keeps the no-reuse boundary.
gaps.boundary: Does not choose a license or provide legal advice.

gaps.title: One permissioned external weak artifact
gaps.nextAction: Collect one user-submitted, permissioned, or clearly redacted weak artifact.
gaps.boundary: Does not create external proof or grant permission.

gaps.title: Completed permissioned before/after case
gaps.nextAction: Run the reviewed artifact through the full case path and preserve the boundary check.
gaps.boundary: Does not prove improvement, adoption, or outcome until the before/after case evidence exists.

gaps.title: npm package publication
gaps.nextAction: After license and sync gates close, owner decides whether npm publication is in scope.
gaps.boundary: Dry-run package checks are not npm publication.

gaps.title: Tagged GitHub Action or Marketplace publication
gaps.nextAction: Owner decides whether a tagged public action release or Marketplace listing is in scope.
gaps.boundary: Repository-local action metadata is not Marketplace publication.

gaps.title: Shipped plugin or connector proof
gaps.nextAction: Produce a real tagged plugin/action release or installation proof before shipped-plugin claims.
gaps.boundary: Local plugin scaffolds and install-readiness packets are not shipped plugins.

gaps.title: Benchmarked productivity evidence
gaps.nextAction: Run the measurement protocol and review the resulting evidence before productivity claims.
gaps.boundary: Measurement protocol only; no benchmark claim without direct evidence.

gaps.title: External adoption evidence
gaps.nextAction: Collect and review real external usage evidence before adoption claims.
gaps.boundary: Read-only public repository visibility is not external adoption.

allowedClaim: Mimesis has a local gap register that keeps remaining owner, proof, publication, benchmark, and adoption gates visible.
disallowedClaim: The gap register does not mean the framework is externally proven, adopted, published, legally licensed for reuse, or complete.
